top_signals.title: Fed meeting minutes to show 'family fight' over rates. The squabble could drag on for a while
top_signals.summary: There have been few instances over the past 35 years or so when the Fed has only made one rate move, be it up or down.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Mobile app Toss and blockchain Optimism to conduct Korean won stablecoin POC : report
top_signals.summary: Toss has reportedly partnered with Optimism and Sunnyside Labs for a proof of concept on the feasibility of a Korean won-based stablecoin for payments.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: FSCS Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements 2025/26
top_signals.summary: In its Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements published today (Thursday 2 July, 2026), the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) outlines the vital work it has carried out during the past financial year.

top_signals.title: Largest wind farm in the United States slated to begin commercial operations
top_signals.summary: The SunZia Wind Project, the largest wind farm in the United States, is slated to begin commercial operations this month. The wind farm, located in New Mexico, has a total net summer generating capacity of 3,650 megawatts (MW) and is composed of 916 wind turbines. SunZia's capacity is more than three times larger than the next two largest wind farms, Alta Wind in Southern California (1,098 MW) and Great Prairie in northern Texas (1,027 MW). The SunZia Wind Project works with a high voltage transmission line to deliver the wind power generated to Arizona and California.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
